expectations.text: Spec has all required sections including Acceptance Criteria
expectations.evidence: Missing '## Acceptance Criteria' entirely. Missing '### Failure Modes' entirely. Present: ## Problem (L3), ## Solution (L8), ## Scope (L14), ## Design Decisions (L29), ## Risks (L155), ## Alternatives (L163), ## Verification (L178).

expectations.text: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Four code fences found in Design Decisions section: ```protobuf at L35, closing ``` at L87, ```protobuf at L91, closing ``` at L105. No Implementation Notes section exists at all. Hard fail.

expectations.text: No TypeScript types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L29-152) contains protobuf syntax (message, repeated, enum, rpc) but no TypeScript-specific tokens like 'interface ', ': string', ': number', ': boolean', or '=> Promise'.

expectations.text: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains full protobuf message definitions: AlertRuleTemplate (L36-59), TemplateVariable (L61-69), ComparisonOperator enum (L71-79), VariableType enum (L81-86), and AlertTemplateService (L92-104). These are data structure definitions — proto shapes.

expectations.text: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope items (L17-22) describe capabilities: 'CRUD API', 'Template picker UI', 'Dashboard panel context menu integration', 'Variable substitution engine', 'Pre-filling from a panel's MetricQuery'. No file paths like 'src/...' or extensions like '.ts'/'.tsx'.
Spec is 193 lines total. No Implementation Notes section exists to exclude. 193 >> 120.

expectations.text: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L29-152) uses subsection headers (### Proto Definition, ### API Surface, etc.) and bold labels (**Alerts module:**, **Dashboard module:**) but paragraphs themselves rarely open with bold declarative decision sentences. L108 has **`InstantiateAlertRule`** and **`MatchTemplates`** as bold labels in bullet points, not decision-as-fact topic sentences. L131, L143, L152 open paragraphs without bold. Well under 80% threshold.

Spec provides full proto definitions (L36-86), complete API surface with 7 RPCs (L92-104), detailed variable substitution algorithm (L131-139), step-by-step dashboard→alert flow (L143-148), storage approach (L152), and cross-module integration contracts (L111-128). Enough detail to start backend and frontend work.

expectations.text: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains: 4 code fences (L35, L87, L91, L105), full protobuf message definitions with field numbers (L36-86), full service RPC definitions (L92-104). These are implementation artifacts, not decision prose. Hard fail.

No ### Failure Modes section exists. The ## Risks table (L156-161) lists risks with mitigations but these are framed as engineering mitigations, not policy decisions the team could disagree on. No Failure Modes section to evaluate.

In Scope (L17-22): 5 items, all behavioral capabilities — 'server-side CRUD API', 'Template picker UI', 'Dashboard panel context menu integration', 'Variable substitution engine for template instantiation', 'Pre-filling from a panel's MetricQuery'. >80% behavioral.
No ## Acceptance Criteria section exists anywhere in the spec. Missing entirely. Hard fail per assertion rules.

Verification (L178-193) has Unit Tests, Integration Tests, and Manual Verification subsections. No Agent-Verifiable subsection. Tests are described narratively ('Variable substitution engine: all variable types, missing required variable errors...') not in action → outcome format. Manual Verification section (L189-193) is explicitly manual. No agent-actionable action→outcome checks.

expectations.text: Spec has an Appetite field in the header
expectations.evidence: Header is just '# Feature Spec: Alert Rule Templates' (L1) followed immediately by '## Problem' (L3). No **Appetite:** field anywhere in the spec. Missing entirely.

expectations.text: Design Decisions use three-tier boundaries where appropriate
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions has scattered constraints ('NOT on its internal state' L122, 'server-side only' L139) but these are incidental technical constraints, not structured boundary tiers providing a decision model for gray areas. No Always/Ask First/Never or Must/Should/Must Not framework. No systematic tier language giving implementers a decision model for ambiguous situations. Zero deliberate boundary-tier language.
